title: psoralea corymbosa
intro: Psoralea corymbosa, also known as Bishop's weed, is a perennial plant native to the Indian subcontinent and Southeast Asia. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help alleviate conditions such as arthritis and improve overall health. The health benefits of Psoralea corymbosa include its ability to support skin health, reduce stress, and boost the immune system. The plant can be prepared as a herbal tea, tincture, or capsule, and is often used in Ayurvedic and traditional Chinese medicine.
